name: Mexican-Style Eggs Benedict
servings: serves 4 | prep. time 20 minutes | cooking time 25-30 minutes

ingredients.item: Salt and black pepper to taste
chorizo sausages, removed from their casings and ground
chopped chipotle pepper in adobo or 1 teaspoon chipotle powder or to taste

method: Peel the squash and slice it into 4 slices of about ½ inch thickness. Brush slices on both sides with olive oil. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Preheat the grill over medium heat. Grease the grates with some vegetable oil.
Add the squash slices. Grill for 5-6 minutes on each side until softened, tender, and the grill marks are visible.
Add the chorizo to a medium saucepan or skillet over medium heat. Cook, breaking the sausage up with a spatula, until crumbly and cooked through. Set aside.
In another medium saucepan or skillet, melt the butter.
In a small mixing bowl, combine the egg yolks, lemon juice, chipotle pepper, and salt.
Add a spoonful of the melted butter and combine well. Repeat until you've used all butter.
In a small saucepan or skillet, pour water until it's ½-inch high. Add a splash of vinegar and a pinch of salt. Heat water over medium-high until bubbly. Do not bring it to a boil.
Crack an egg into a small bowl (do not whisk). Gently add the egg to the heated water. The egg white will start to firm up gradually. Cook for about 3-4 minutes until the white is well-set.
Using a spoon, remove the cooked egg and transfer it over a paper towel to drain excess water.
Cook the remaining eggs the same way.
Arrange the squash slices on plates. Top each with mashed avocado and chorizo.
Add the poached eggs to each and drizzle with the sauce. Top with the jalapeño and cilantro.
